subject: Geography
title: Island Gigantism and Dwarfism
description: A quick win: understand Island Gigantism and Dwarfism.

hook.text: On islands, small animals tend to evolve larger and large animals tend to shrink.

buildup.text: Komodo dragons grew to three meters long on Indonesian islands with few competitors.

discovery.text: Homo floresiensis, a meter-tall human species, lived on the island of Flores until 50,000 years ago.

twist.text: Dwarf elephants once roamed Mediterranean islands—shrunk to the size of large dogs.

climax.text: Islands act as evolutionary labs where the normal rules of size get rewritten.

punchline.text: Islands shrink the big and grow the small.

quiz.question: What is the 'island rule' in biology?
options: Small animals get bigger and large animals get smaller
Animals lose the ability to fly
